name: PINEAL CYST
definition: small nonneoplastic cyst of pineal gland

demographics.Path: uni- / multilocular cyst; inner layer = gliotic tissue, middle layer = pineal parenchymal
demographics.Size: 2-15 mm, remaining stable over time; may be symptomatic if > 15 mm

dominant_finding: normal-sized gland (80%), slightly > 1 cm in 20%, can be > 2 cm in size
distribution: appearance of a solid mass on delayed contrast images (contrast may diffuse from enhanced
discriminating_features: normal-sized gland (80%), slightly > 1 cm in 20%, can be > 2 cm in size
isodense to CSF in surrounding cistern (infrequently noted)
isointense to CSF on T1WI; may have higher signal intensity than CSF ← high protein

CT: normal-sized gland (80%), slightly > 1 cm in 20%, can be > 2 cm in size
isodense to CSF in surrounding cistern (infrequently noted)
MR: isointense to CSF on T1WI; may have higher signal intensity than CSF ← high protein
slightly hyperintense to CSF on T2WI ← phase coherence in cyst but not in moving CSF
signal not completely suppressed on FLAIR ← proteinaceous content
typically incomplete enhancement of cyst wall ← fragmentation of pineal parenchyma as
appearance of a solid mass on delayed contrast images (contrast may diffuse from enhanced
slight impression on superior colliculi (sagittal image)

clinical.Prognosis: lack of growth over 9 years (in 75%); rarely "pineal apoplexy" = intracystic
